data.description:
## Description

Write a failing RED integration test for the `ticket summary` subcommand before the implementation exists.

## File Impact
- tests/scripts/test-ticket-summary-dispatcher.sh (new — note: test-issue-summary.sh already tests the script directly; this file tests dispatcher routing + new behaviors)
- .test-index (modify — append to `plugins/dso/scripts/ticket` and `plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-lib-api.sh` entries)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tests/scripts/test-ticket-summary-dispatcher.sh exists and is executable (chmod +x)
- [ ] Test invokes `ticket summary <id>` through the dispatcher (not issue-summary.sh directly)
- [ ] Test asserts: output format matches "<id> [<status>] <title> (ready)" for a ticket with no blockers
- [ ] Test asserts: output format matches "<id> [<status>] <title> (blocked by: <dep-ids>)" for a blocked ticket
- [ ] Test asserts: circular block relationships do NOT cause infinite loops (test with A blocks B, B blocks A; must return within timeout e.g. 5s)
- [ ] Test asserts: dangling link references (dep pointing to non-existent ticket) produce a warning in output rather than crashing (exit 0, no crash)
- [ ] Tests FAIL (RED) before implementation task T2 runs
- [ ] .test-index updated to add test file to both dispatcher and lib entries

## Red Marker
tests/scripts/test-ticket-summary-dispatcher.sh [test_summary_cycle_detection_through_dispatcher]

## Notes
Use mock TICKET_CMD to inject fixture data. For the cycle detection test: mock ticket A shows "DEPENDS ON" section pointing to B, mock ticket B shows "DEPENDS ON" section pointing to A. The implementation must detect the cycle and break the loop, outputting a summary with a warning rather than hanging. For dangling link test: mock a ticket whose dep ID causes ticket show to return empty/error — the summary should gracefully output a warning like "(blocked by: <id> [dangling])" rather than crashing.
data.title: [RED] Write failing integration test for ticket summary dispatcher
